steps.title: Identify what makes barter inefficient
steps.content: The notes quote Champ and Freeman on the core requirement of barter: “the person with whom you wish to trade must not only have what you want but also want what you have.” This mutual alignment of desires explains why barter is inefficient — it's the root cause of the problem.

steps.title: Match the explanation to the answers
steps.content:
The correct response paraphrases this requirement directly as “the precise alignment of desires that barter economies require” and connects it to inefficiency.

The other three either avoid explaining the cause of inefficiency or shift the focus away from explaining it.

That confirms A is correct.
